back_translation: And it happened afterward, that the king of the sons of Ammon died, and Hanun his son reigned in his place.

And David said, 'I will do steadfast love with Hanun son of Nahash, as his father did with me steadfast love.' And David sent to console him by the hand of his servants concerning his father; and the servants of David came to the land of the sons of Ammon.

And the princes of the sons of Ammon said to Hanun their lord, 'Is David honoring your father in your eyes, that he has sent to you comforters? Has not David sent his servants to you for the sake of searching out the city and spying it out and overthrowing it?'
And Hanun took the servants of David and shaved off half of their beards, and cut off their garments in half, as far as their hips, and sent them away.
And they told David, and he sent to meet them, for the men had been greatly humiliated. And the king said, 'Stay in Jericho until your beards grow, and then return.'

And the sons of Ammon saw that they had become a stench to David; and the sons of Ammon sent and hired the Arameans of Beth-rehob and the Arameans of Zoba — twenty thousand foot soldiers — and the king of Maacah with a thousand men, and the men of Tob, twelve thousand men.
And David heard, and sent Joab and all the army of the mighty men.
And the sons of Ammon came out and arrayed for battle at the entrance of the gate; and the Arameans of Zoba and Rehob, and the men of Tob and Maacah, by themselves in the field.
And Joab saw that the faces of the battle were against him from front and from behind; and he chose from all the chosen ones of Israel and arrayed against Aram.
And the rest of the people he gave into the hand of Abishai his brother, and he arrayed against the sons of Ammon.
And he said, 'If Aram is too strong for me, then you will be to me for salvation; and if the sons of Ammon are too strong for you, then I will come to save you.
Be strong, and let us be strong for the sake of our people and for the cities of our God; and the LORD shall do what is good in His eyes.'
And Joab and the people who were with him drew near to the battle against Aram, and they fled before him.
And the sons of Ammon saw that Aram had fled, and they too fled before Abishai, and entered the city. And Joab returned from the sons of Ammon and came to Jerusalem.
And Aram saw that they had been defeated before Israel, and they gathered together.
And Hadadezer sent and brought out Aram that was from beyond the river, and they came to Helam; and Shobach, commander of the army of Hadadezer, was before them.
And it was told to David, and he gathered all Israel and crossed the Jordan and came to Helam; and Aram arrayed to meet David and fought with him.
And Aram fled before Israel, and David killed of Aram seven hundred chariots and forty thousand horsemen; and Shobach the commander of his army he struck, and he died there.
And all the kings, the servants of Hadadezer, saw that they had been defeated before Israel, and they made peace with Israel and served them. And Aram was afraid to save the sons of Ammon any more.
